results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THE PROJECT IS TO INFORM PUBLIC HEALTH GUIDANCE ON IF (BASED ON SCHOOL AND NEARBY HOME CHARACTERISTICS) AND WHEN (I.E., AT WHAT AIR QUALITY INDEX (AQI) OR PM2.5 CONCENTRATION LEVELS) SCHOOLS SHOULD CLOSE DURING WILDFIRE SMOKE EVENTS TO PROTECT THE HEALTH OF STUDENTS. ACTIVITIES:TO ACHIEVE THE GOAL, RESEARCHERS WILL: 1)  INVESTIGATE FACTORS THAT INFLUENCE THE OUTDOOR TO INDOOR TRANSPORT OF PM2.5 INTO SCHOOLS AND STUDENTS' HOMES DURING WILDFIRE EVENTS; AND 2) ASSESS THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN PM2.5 LEVELS INSIDE AND OUTSIDE OF SCHOOLS AND CHILDREN'S RESPIRATORY HEALTH.  SUBRECIPIENT:OUTCOMES:DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROGRAM INCLUDE THE SCIENTIFIC COMMUNITY, STATE AND LOCAL ENTITIES WORKING WITH SCHOOL DISTRICTS, AND SCHOOL-AGED CHILDREN AND THEIR FAMILIES.
